description: Main navigation always visible or easily accessible. Never hide critical nav behind hamburger on desktop.
Show user location in hierarchy. Essential for deep content sites (ecommerce, docs, legal).
If site has >20 pages, search should be in header. Auto-suggest and recent searches.
Primary actions in bottom nav bar for thumb reach. Max 5 items.
Show only relevant fields. Use conditional logic to reveal sections as needed.
Validate fields on blur, not just on submit. Show success states too.
Pre-fill with likely values. Auto-detect country, currency, timezone.
Never clear the form on error. Highlight the specific field. Scroll to first error.
One column forms have 15% higher completion. Reserve multi-column for related short fields only.
Show content shape during load, not spinners. Reduces perceived wait time by 50%.
Show the result immediately, sync in background. Essential for chat, likes, toggles.
For operations >2 seconds, show determinate progress. For >10 seconds, show time estimate.
Load visible content first. Defer below-fold images and components.
Success/info messages via toasts (auto-dismiss 3-5s). Errors persist until dismissed.
Subtle animations on interactions. Button press scales, cards lift on hover, smooth transitions.
Never show blank pages. Every empty state gets illustration, explanation, and primary action.
Only for destructive/irreversible actions. Never for routine operations.
45-75 characters per line for body text. Use max-width: 65ch on text containers.
Consistent spacing using a base unit (4px or 8px grid). Margins are multiples of base.
Clear size steps: h1 (2.5rem), h2 (2rem), h3 (1.5rem), h4 (1.25rem). Never skip levels.
16px minimum for body text on all devices. 14px only for labels and captions.
4.5:1 minimum for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18px+). Test with WebAIM checker.
Color should never be the ONLY indicator. Add icons, text, or patterns alongside.
System-preference detection. CSS variables for easy theme switching. Test both modes.
Red=destructive, Green=success, Yellow=warning, Blue=info. Never violate conventions.
Place key info top-left. Users scan in F-pattern on text-heavy pages.
Hero (top-left to right), diagonal scan, CTA (bottom-left to right) for marketing pages.
Same card height per row. Use aspect-ratio or min-height. Never jagged grids.
When in doubt, add more space. Cramped layouts feel untrustworthy.
320px (small phone), 375px (phone), 768px (tablet), 1024px (laptop), 1280px (desktop).
Minimum 44x44px for all tappable elements. 8px minimum gap between targets.
Every clickable element needs distinct hover state. Cursor change alone insufficient.
Visible focus rings for keyboard nav. Never remove :focus-visible outlines.
Smooth scroll for anchor links. Preserve scroll position on back navigation.
Draggable items need visual grip handles. Show drop zones on drag start.
WebP/AVIF formats. Responsive srcset. Lazy load below-fold images.
font-display: swap to prevent FOIT. Preload critical fonts. Max 3 font files.
Code-split by route. Dynamic import heavy components. Tree-shake unused code.
LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1. Measure with Lighthouse.
Use nav, main, article, section, aside. Screen readers depend on structure.
Descriptive alt text for informational images. Empty alt for decorative images.
All functionality accessible via keyboard. Tab order follows visual flow.
Label all interactive elements. aria-label for icon buttons. aria-live for dynamic content.
Skip to main content link as first focusable element. Hidden until focused.
Primary actions in bottom third of screen. Secondary in middle. Tertiary at top.
Support swipe for carousels, dismissing modals, navigating tabs. Add visual hints.
Always set viewport meta tag. Never disable zoom (accessibility violation).
Use touch-action: manipulation to remove 300ms tap delay on mobile.
Reviews, testimonials, user counts near conversion points. Not buried in footer.
SSL badge, payment security logos near forms. Privacy policy link in footer.
Contact info, legal links, social media, company info. Sparse footers feel untrustworthy.
